title: Demand destruction sequence
description: Oil prices can fall while activity, petroleum demand, and employment conditions worsen when falling demand drives the decline.
plainLanguageSummary: Oil prices can fall while activity, petroleum demand, and employment conditions worsen when falling demand drives the decline.
howToRead: Compare direction, turning points, and sustained divergences. Use the tooltip for exact values and observation dates; visual alignment alone does not establish causation.

calculation.formula: WTI, petroleum consumption, and industrial production use year-over-year change; unemployment is a published level.
calculation.explanation: A falling oil price can also reflect supply growth, efficiency, currency moves, or policy rather than demand destruction.
calculation.example: A value at date t uses only observations dated t or earlier unless the chart explicitly labels a visual shift.

patternsToWatch: Turning points or divergences that repeat across historical episodes
limitations: A falling oil price can also reflect supply growth, efficiency, currency moves, or policy rather than demand destruction.

methodology.formula: WTI, petroleum consumption, and industrial production use year-over-year change; unemployment is a published level.
methodology.notes: A falling oil price can also reflect supply growth, efficiency, currency moves, or policy rather than demand destruction.
